rationale: GQA-to-MLA transition for high-concurrency financial LLMs on Ascend is a real inference-cost technique for FS deployment, but Huawei-ecosystem lock-in and prior coverage limit immediate practitioner utility.

Introduces 'Granularity Gap' framing for sycophancy evals across Gemini generations with 73 adversarial prompts—useful for teams building LLM guardrails, but prior coverage and no code/repo limit freshness and utility.

ProSPy's four-stage agentic Text-to-SQL pipeline for large heterogeneous enterprise schemas is directly relevant to FS data teams querying complex internal databases, but prior coverage and no public repo limit freshness and drop-in utility.

Novel SIR-based framework for cross-model synthetic data contamination is theoretically interesting and shifts how leaders think about corpus hygiene at scale, but offers no code/tool/benchmark to act on this week.
CHASE introduces RL-driven adversarial red-blue teaming to harden LLMs against black-box prompt attacks, a real safety alignment advance, but prior coverage exists and no code/repo is yet available.

LatentSkill converts agent textual skills into LoRA adapters via hypernetwork, reducing context overhead and hiding skill content—a concrete technique for agent builders, but prior coverage of skill-quality issues limits freshness.

LANTERN is a Tier-1 RAG/context-engineering paper with a concrete benchmark (78.3% fact recovery, <25ms, zero LLM calls) but has prior coverage and no code/repo yet surfaced, limiting immediate adoption.
Proposes latent-space inter-agent communication to cut token cost and info loss in MAS—Tier-1 agentic topic but no code/repo yet and prior coverage exists, limiting immediate utility.

Predictable scaling laws for continued pre-training hyperparameters (LR, batch size) could replace costly grid searches for teams fine-tuning domain LLMs, but no code/repo yet and prior coverage reduces freshness.
ReasoningFlow DAG-based annotation schema for LRM traces is a useful eval/monitoring contribution but lacks code release and has prior coverage, limiting immediate practitioner uptake.
Automated schema discovery for multi-source RAG over heterogeneous data is a real Tier-1 RAG technique, but no code/repo is surfaced and prior coverage exists, limiting actionability this week.

New agent-planning benchmark for adaptive re-planning under dual constraints is Tier-1 relevant but hits only one signal dimension (tomorrow/anticipate); no code/dataset confirmed, prior coverage reduces freshness.

Graph-based RAG for hallucination reduction is Tier-1 territory but this is a long-form repost of prior-covered work (c_16caccb2b6e1e4b5) with no new code, benchmark, or capability signal to act on this week.

AURA's IntentFrame mechanism for implicit-need surfacing in tool-use agents is a genuine Tier-1 agentic idea, but it's a single arXiv paper with no code/repo, a narrow 100-query benchmark, and zero practical adoption path this week.
EMBER addresses a real agentic memory bottleneck (pre-query evidence budgeting) but is arxiv-only with no code/benchmark release, and prior coverage reduces freshness value.

IA-RAG extends RAG with interval-algebra temporal constraints (duration, overlap, containment) — a real Tier-1 technique, but no code/repo yet and prior coverage already exists, limiting freshness and utility.

Tier-1 agentic RAG paper proposing memory-decoupled multi-agent long-doc QA, but no code/repo, prior coverage exists, and practical adoption path is weak this week.

Tier-1 agentic AI paper on skill specification/generalization for inference-time improvement, but no code/repo yet and prior coverage exists; hits 'tomorrow' signal only weakly.
